---
title: "Week 1 — AI fluency foundations"
subtitle: "The 4 D's: Delegation, Description, Discernment, Diligence"
---

## Topics

- What "AI fluency" means, and why it matters for biology research.
- The 4 D's framework, following [Anthropic's AI Fluency: Framework & Foundations](https://www.anthropic.com/ai-fluency).
- First pass at applying the framework to your own workflow.
- Course tooling: Quarto + a pinned environment + a coding assistant.

## Before class

Read:

- [Delegation](../fluency/delegation.qmd)
- [Description](../fluency/description.qmd)
- [Discernment](../fluency/discernment.qmd)
- [Diligence](../fluency/diligence.qmd)

Skim:

- Anthropic, [*AI Fluency: Framework & Foundations*](https://www.anthropic.com/ai-fluency) — overview page.

## In class

- Introductions and workflow audit.
- Live walkthrough: the 4 D's applied to a generic delegation example (e.g., "extract structured information from a methods section" — works across subfields).
- Tooling segment (~30 min): set up Quarto, a pinned environment of your choice (`renv` / `conda` / `uv`), and a coding assistant. By the end of class you'll have a working `quarto preview` and one committed reproducible report skeleton.
- Small-group exercise: classify three tasks from your own work using the delegation rubric — **without** consulting an AI yet.

## After class

- **Deliverable** *(part of weekly exercises, 30%)*: a short reflection (≤ 500 words):
  1. **AI-free first pass**: classify three tasks from your own work using the 4 D's rubric, *without* AI input. Note your reasoning.
  2. **AI-second pass**: optionally, run the same three tasks past an AI assistant. Note what changed in your classification, what you accepted, and what you rejected.
  3. **Tooling confirmation**: paste the URL of your `quarto preview` skeleton repo, or attach the rendered HTML.
  4. **Disclosure statement** following the [rubric](../course-syllabus.qmd#disclosure-rubric).

The AI-free first pass is the point: the goal is to develop *your* classification skill, not to recognize when an AI's classification is reasonable.

## Supplementary

Slides, recording, and notebooks will be posted here after each session.
